# aarch.science

> Native arm64 (aarch64) containers for the conda-forge scientific stack —
> geospatial first — for Apple Silicon Macs and AWS Graviton. cosign-signed,
> public, no account required. `docker pull quay.io/aarchsci/geospatial:latest`
> and it runs native: no QEMU emulation, no `exec format error`.

aarch.science solves a delivery gap, not a porting problem: conda-forge already
ships `linux-aarch64` packages for the scientific stack (GDAL, PROJ, GEOS,
rasterio, shapely, scikit-image), but there is no curated, verified set of
*containers* built from them. The stack also famously won't `pip install` on
arm64 (`No matching distribution found for rasterio`) even though it solves clean
on conda-forge. aarch.science builds curated conda-forge environments native for
arm64 and publishes them signed.

It is the sister project to aarch.bio (which does this for bioinformatics /
bioconda). Same machinery; conda-forge science layer instead.

## The crucial difference from a solve: verified to RUN

An environment can *solve* and still fail to *assemble/import* on arm64 (missing
native lib, ABI mismatch, broken wheel). So every aarch.science image is tested
inside the built arm64 container before it is tagged: it must import every
headline package AND pass a functional smoke test (reproject a point with PROJ,
GEOS geometry ops, write+read a GeoTIFF through rasterio and raw GDAL, round-trip
a GeoJSON through fiona). The smoke test is baked into the image:
`docker run --rm quay.io/aarchsci/geospatial:latest python /opt/aarchsci/smoke.py`.

## Use it

- Direct pull: `docker pull quay.io/aarchsci/<env>:latest`
- Available envs:
  - `geospatial` (123 packages — gdal, proj, geos, rasterio, fiona, shapely,
    pyproj, scikit-image, numpy, scipy, + deps, all native arm64).
  - `earth-observation` (236 packages — the geospatial base plus xarray, dask,
    rioxarray, stackstac, pystac-client, odc-stac, netcdf4, zarr, all native arm64).
- Tags: `latest`, a date tag (e.g. `2026.06.25`), and a content-addressed
  `s<lock-hash>` tag (the hash of the resolved package set).
- Works on Apple Silicon and AWS Graviton (linux/arm64).

## Verify (don't trust)

Every image is cosign keyless-signed and logged in the Sigstore transparency log:
```
cosign verify quay.io/aarchsci/geospatial:latest \
  --certificate-identity-regexp 'github.com/playgroundlogic/aarchsci' \
  --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com
```

## Scope

In scope: the CPU scientific stack on conda-forge (geospatial / earth-observation
first; geo-ml, climate later). Curated environment images, not a registry mirror.
Out of scope: GPU/CUDA (Graviton has no NVIDIA GPU — a hardware reality, not a
packaging one), bioinformatics (that's aarch.bio), and compiling from source
(gaps go upstream to conda-forge).

## Key links

- Website: https://aarch.science/
- Registry (browse all images): https://quay.io/organization/aarchsci
- Source & docs: https://github.com/playgroundlogic/aarchsci
- Design & decisions: https://github.com/playgroundlogic/aarchsci/blob/main/DESIGN.md
- Sister project: https://aarch.bio/

## Facts

- Images: `geospatial` (123 pkgs) and `earth-observation` (236 pkgs) — native
  arm64, verified (assemble+import+functional) + signed + public.
- Kept current by a daily reconciler that re-solves each env spec and rebuilds
  when the resolved package set (its lock-hash) changes.
- Maintained by Playground Logic. Apache-2.0. Unofficial — not affiliated with or
  endorsed by conda-forge, Anaconda, or AWS.
